title: molinia caerulea subsp. arundinacea
intro: Molinia caerulea subsp. arundinacea, also known as purple moor-grass, is a perennial grass native to the UK and parts of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of purple moor-grass include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of purple moor-grass can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
